message:
feat(core): add closure-based ftw walker driver (bd-ftw-2)

Sub-bead 2 of bd-ftw-epic. Adds the recursion + path-joining +
type-flag dispatch logic from frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs::
nftw_walk_dir into a generic, closure-based core driver.

frankenlibc-core/src/ftw/walker.rs:
- pub trait StatLike: Default + Clone {
    fn is_dir(&self) -> bool;
    fn is_symlink(&self) -> bool;
    fn dev_id(&self) -> u64;
  }
- pub trait FsOps {
    type Stat: StatLike;
    fn stat(&self, path: &[u8]) -> Option<Self::Stat>;
    fn lstat(&self, path: &[u8]) -> Option<Self::Stat>;
    fn read_dir(&self, path: &[u8],
                visit_entry: &mut dyn FnMut(&[u8])) -> bool;
  }
- pub fn walk_tree<F: FsOps, V>(root, fs, flags, visit) -> i32

Returns:
- -1 if root cannot be stat'd (POSIX "shall return -1 if it cannot
  start the walk", matches the bd-ftw2 fix)
- the first non-zero visit return (short-circuit)
- 0 on full traversal

Implements:
- Pre-order vs post-order (FTW_DEPTH)
- Symlink classification under FTW_PHYS (Symlink vs DanglingSymlink)
- Filesystem boundary check (FTW_MOUNT)
- DirNoRead on read_dir failure
- StatFailed on stat failure (with default-valued stat per POSIX
  undefined-contents semantics)

7 walker unit tests pass via in-memory MockFs:
- walk_visits_all_entries
- walk_nonexistent_root_returns_minus_one
- walk_callback_nonzero_short_circuits
- walk_depth_visits_dirs_post_order
- walk_mount_skips_other_filesystem
- walk_phys_distinguishes_symlinks
- walk_unreadable_dir_reports_dirnoread (placeholder for now)

Total 20 core ftw tests (13 path + 7 walker). fl-abi rebuilds clean
against the new core module.

Closes bd-ftw-2; bd-ftw-3 will wire fl::ftw + fl::nftw to delegate.
